description_long: This is a small, single-engine airplane with a high-wing configuration and a bright red and white color scheme. The fuselage is streamlined and features a forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with clear windows, likely accommodating one or two seats. The wings are straight and mounted above the fuselage, with visible struts for structural support. The tail section has a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear is fixed in a tricycle arrangement, with two main wheels and a nose wheel. The propeller is mounted at the nose, indicating a piston engine typical of light general aviation aircraft.
description: A small, red and white high-wing airplane with a single nose-mounted propeller, fixed tricycle landing gear, and a conventional tail. The cockpit is forward and enclosed, with clear windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the high-mounted straight wings, nose propeller, and fixed tricycle landing gear.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the red fuselage, 'NYGS' marking, wing struts, and the single propeller at the nose.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, as well as the full wingspan.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the cockpit windows, wing struts, and tail design.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with external struts and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and one nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with clear side windows, likely single or dual seat.
